name: Buster Williams
abstract: Charles Anthony "Buster" Williams (born April 17, 1942) is an American jazz bassist. Williams is known for his membership in pianist Herbie Hancock's early 1970s group, as well as working with guitarist Larry Coryell, the Thelonious Monk repertory band Sphere and as the accompanist of choice for many singers, including Nancy Wilson.

interviews.title: Buster Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: we had the trio, Ben Riley, Buster Williams, and myself. We had been part of the Ron Carter quartet, that rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Denny Zeitlin
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got a number of trio performances coming up in the next year, most of them will be with Buster Williams on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Denny Zeitlin interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Mickey Roker
about_quotes.evidence: Me and Buster Williams was the rhythm section and a piano and a conductor named Don Trenner.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mickey Roker interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I organized a group and we did some concerts. The group was Gary Bartz on alto saxophone, Billy Harper on tenor saxophone, Kenny Barron, Buster Williams, and Idris Mohammed on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We started doing a group called the Timeless Allstars, which was put together by Cedar [Walton] and Harold, Curtis Fuller, Billy, Buster, traveling all over Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: Buster was part of a later group called The Great Jazz Trio. Buster was in that along with Grady Tate, as the drummer, I believe if I'm not mistaken.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
